title: Unused assignments should be removed
description: Unused assignments should be removedAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalmaintainabilityCode Smellcwesymbolic-executioncertunusedUnused assignments should be removed.Why is this an issue?What is the potential impact?How can I fix it?More InfoComputing or retrieving a value only to then immediately overwrite it or throw it away indicates a serious logic error in the code.Assigning a value to a local variable that is not read by any subsequent instruction is called adead store. The following code snippet depicts a few dead stores.int foo() { int x = 0; // Noncompliant: dead store, next line overwrites x x = 100; // Noncompliant: dead store, next line overwrites x x = 200; int y = 0; y += 9001; // Noncompliant: dead store, y is never used int z = 300; // Noncompliant: dead store, next line
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

checks.message: Unused assignments should be removed. Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.
